REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (192 Nueva Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94134)
Off Calendar Dec-07-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for Trustee.
Russell Stanaland for and with Ronnie Reddick (Objector/Beneficiary).

Mr. Reddick stated that he made an offer on the house, that he was not told he needed to put down cash on a house he half owns and that they never responded to his proposal. Mr. Stanaland asked if it would be possible for his Client to do a credit bid on the house. The Court stated that a credit bid is not in the terms of the sale. Ms. Friedeberg stated that she did not get a preapproval letter, that they did not sign off on the disclosure and that she could not accept the offer because it is not permissible by law. The Court overruled the objection, informed Mr. Reddick that he can participate in the bidding process, that he needs to have a cash deposit and proceeded with the sale. The sale was confirmed at $750,500.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed October 24, 2017. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $863,000.00 is required.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Off Calendar Sep-11-2017 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-18-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PCN-03-284363 Conservatorship of Bessie Dedeaux and PTR-07-289706 The Bessie Dedeaux Trust.
Appearances: Brent Kato for the (conservator & trustee) John Halley.

The Court stated that this is the final report in the conservatorship and the 5th accounting in the trust, that there are questions having to do with the fees requested, that it is unusual to see a son request full fees for visiting and spending holiday's with his mother, that there is a concern regarding the conservator charging as conservator for building maintenance items and that he billed a high number of hours for tasks that should not have taken that long. Mr. Kato stated that he forwarded the examiner's notes to his client, that the bills were in 1 hour increments but rounded down, that the conservator resides in Oakland but the property is in South San Francisco, that the same issues arose in the last accounting, that the conservator does the maintenance work on his own which is a savings and that he and his wife visit often to get his mother to socialize and participate in programs. The Court stated that it seems as if a son would spend holiday's with his mother. Mr. Kato stated that the conservator is the only one who visits and that no other family members visit. The court stated that the issue is not preserving the funds for the conservatee but for the beneficiaries. Mr. Kato stated that the only asset left is the house, that the client and a nephew are the only beneficiaries and addressed the staff costs and that they were for paralegal time and getting courtesy copies to the examiners. The accounting petitions in both cases were taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)
